problem.summary: Compute a 256-bin histogram from uint32 input values. The baseline atomically increments global bins for every element. The optimized seed privatizes the 256 bins in shared memory per block, then merges block-private bin counts into global memory.
outputs: histogram[0:256] contains exact counts for input[i] & 255
constraints: counts are expected to fit in uint32_t for benchmarked shapes
input distribution strongly affects atomic contention and should be recorded with every result

verification.method: CPU reference histogram over the same generated input, exact per-bin comparison, and total-count check. The 16777216-element skewed-input gfx1201 run passed with mismatched_bins=0 and max_abs_error=0.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. On 2026-05-28, AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT gfx1201 HIP-event timing measured 0.66270 ms baseline versus 0.05980 ms optimized for a 16777216-element skewed 256-bin histogram, speedup 11.081940x. No rocprofiler/rocprof counters were collected; the optimized run includes one implausibly tiny min sample, so use median and p10/p90 rather than best-of timing.
